ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩya hʋ aa kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋbɔŋlɔrɩnɩ, tɩya hʋ mɛ aa bɩ kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋweliye.
translated: The one who has a child should not say that his child will never be sick, and the one who does not have a child should not say that his child will never die.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Wɩɩsɩbee koro hʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ bayalʋʋ aa gɔllɩ kɩ kyɛ sɩfɩyaŋbʋyɩ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ pɛɛl.
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba aa hɔnɔ kɩ di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ a kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa kyaŋ. Ŋ teŋbii rɛ nyɛ.”
translated: He said to them, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ kaŋ baal hʋ kɩ kyele aŋ kyɩɩrɩ yʋga aŋ lɩɩ baal hʋ tɩyaŋ.
translated: The man who was bitten by a dog is the one who is afraid of the dog.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.

translated: The one who is not taught by others is like a child who has no mother, the one who is taught is like a child who has a mother.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.

translated: The man who has a wife and children is more respected than the man who has none.

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Ba aa sɩŋ kɩ basɩ wɩya hʋ gɛɛ, nala yʋga fá ko kɩ laŋŋɩ. Yesu aa ko na nɩgyamaa hʋ, ʋ basɩ tɩya gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ abee sɩfɩyasɩ a baa, “Gyɩŋganɩɩ, mɩyaŋ nɛ baa dɩ ɩ lɩɩ biye hʋ tɩyaŋ. Kɩ pɔ dɩ ɩ ta bɩl maakyiye kana ʋ.”
translated: He was not willing to go away, but the people urged him to go. Jesus then told his disciples, “Let us go to the other side of the lake.” So they left the crowd and got into the boat.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: Ba kasɩ ba a ŋmaa kɩdɩgɩ, a kpʋ kɩdɩgɩ, aŋ yaga kɩdɩgɩ hʋ mɛ abee bʋwa.
translated: When someone eats the fruit of the kɩdɩgɩ tree, they pick the fruit, chew it and then spit out the seeds.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Doŋ nɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ mɛ a baa, ‘Gyaaŋbiye, ɩ yaa tʋŋtʋnnɩtɩɩ rɛ. Ɩ kɔnɩ daga nɩɩ ɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ tɩɩna rɛ. Ɩ aa wuwo deŋ kɩbiye nyuu weliŋ nyɛ ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ɩ kɩ deŋ kɩgyamaa. Ŋ teŋ fɩyɛla rɛ weliŋ abee wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa yaa. Ɛɛwɩya ŋ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ teŋ mɛ fɩyɛlɩ rɛ a beel-ŋ.’
translated: Then his father said to him, ‘My son, you are always making noise. You never sit still. You have grown up now and you should know that you are no longer a child. I want you to go and look for something to do. Tomorrow I will give you something to go and do.’

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-dɔŋtɩɩna hʋ mɛ tuu gbinni a sʋla ʋ a baa, ‘Di kenyiri tɩya ŋ, ŋ sɩ tuŋ kyɩŋ hʋ.’
translated: If a person is a liar and you tell him a secret, he will say, ‘Don’t tell anyone, I won’t say it.’

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ baal hʋ dɩgɩna suri. Ʋ naŋdeliŋ mɛ bɩrɩmɩ, ʋ kɩ basɩ wɩya weliŋ.
translated: He went to the farm but didn’t work. He just sat down and ate kenkey.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba aa hɔnɔ kɩ di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ a kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa kyaŋ. Ŋ teŋbii rɛ nyɛ.”
translated: He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the good news to everyone. Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.

translated: The man who has a wife and does not take care of her is not a real man.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.

translated: This is how the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about: His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit. Joseph, a native of Nazareth

ref: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
text: Ɩ tɩya kɩ hɔllɩ nala. Ɩ kɩ kyogi nala fene, a kɩ daga ɩ tɩɩ, a kɩ yaa bambugi wɩya.
translated: He said he would come tomorrow. He said he would bring some yams, and then he went away and never returned.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Ma kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa daga anɩɩ ma bɩrɩma lɩɩ ma wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ nɛ,
translated: If you want to go and leave us, let us know so that we can prepare ourselves.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩya hʋ aa kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋbɔŋlɔrɩnɩ, tɩya hʋ mɛ aa bɩ kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋweliye.
translated: One who has a good heart does not remember the bad things done to him, but one who has a bad heart does not forget the good things done to him.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Wɩɩsɩbee koro hʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ bayalʋʋ aa gɔllɩ kɩ kyɛ sɩfɩyaŋbʋyɩ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ pɛɛl.
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba aa hɔnɔ kɩ di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ a kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa kyaŋ. Ŋ teŋbii rɛ nyɛ.”
translated: Then he said to them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ kaŋ baal hʋ kɩ kyele aŋ kyɩɩrɩ yʋga aŋ lɩɩ baal hʋ tɩyaŋ.
translated: The man who owns the house is the one who built it, and the man who built it is the one who owns it.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.

translated: If I had known that you would do this to me, I would not have loved you.
